Added flake.lock file (#8)

* Add V1 bootstrap project scaffold

Initial bootstrap for the drone-platform V1 monorepo. Adds repository skeleton including docs and ADRs, profiles, topologies, example inventory and deployments, interface contracts, adapter and mission-manager stubs, infra skeletons (Ansible, Docker Compose), Nix dev shell (flake.nix), CI workflow, helper scripts (bootstrap and config validation), license, and README. Provides minimal validation tooling and CI wiring to ensure config model completeness and to serve as a starting point for implementing autonomy, adapters, and deployment automation.

Add Python section to .editorconfig to enforce 4-space indentation. Enable host_key_checking in infra/ansible/ansible.cfg. Add an extra profile validation in ops/scripts/validate-config.sh to ensure deployments/full_sitl__split_device.yaml uses profile: full_sitl. Rename rullset.json to ruleset.json (fix typo). Add telemetry_stack role binding to topologies/split_device.yaml.

* Add config validator, CI job, and infra docs

Add a Python config validator and integrate it into CI: introduce ops/scripts/validate-config.py and a new GitHub Actions workflow (config-validation.yml), update CI to install pyyaml and run the validator, and make the shell script call the new Python validator. Provide infra skeletons and docs: add ansible role placeholders, ansible site/inventory templates, docker-compose base file, and architecture docs. commit.message:
Fix board generation: guaranteed parity + strong solver

Three root causes of unsolvable boards:

1. No parity constraint — random digits produced odd complement-group
   counts ({1,9}, {2,8}, etc.), making boards mathematically unclearable
   without appends. Fix: genBoard() generates cells in pairs (equal or
   sum-to-10), guaranteeing even counts for every complement group.

2. samplePairs() only found 6 moves (one per starting cell), so the
   solver explored a tiny fraction of the move space. Fix: use full
   findPairs() (O(n^2) is fine for 24-96 cells in a worker).

3. Accepted boards on first success out of 30 attempts — a 3% random
   success rate is impossibly hard for humans. Fix: require a minimum
   success rate (20% for Intro, 8% for Standard, 3% for Hard, 2% for
   Expert) across 100 greedy-random attempts.

Verified quality — random-play win rates:
  Intro:    80% (was ~3%)
  Standard: 8.8%
  Hard:     3.4%
  Expert:   1.0%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: simplify weekly-build.yml using reusable workflows (workflow_call) (#711)

* Initial plan

* refactor: simplify weekly-build.yml using reusable workflows (workflow_call)

Replace the manual dispatch+poll+watch orchestration in weekly-build.yml
with GitHub's native reusable workflow mechanism:

- release.yml and flatpak.yml: add workflow_call trigger
- release.yml: use inputs.tag (works for both workflow_dispatch and workflow_call)
- weekly-build.yml: replace single job with three clean jobs:
  * release: calls release.yml via uses:
  * flatpak: calls flatpak.yml via uses:
  * notify: reads needs.*.result, collects artifacts from unified
    GITHUB_RUN_ID, sends Slack notification

Eliminates ~90 lines of custom dispatch/poll/watch shell code.

commit.message:
Print proxy configuration on startup if used (#1089)

When a proxy is configured, there was no visibility into the active
proxy settings at server startup, making it difficult to confirm proxy
is picked up correctly.

## Changes

- **`server/server.js`**: Imports `getProxyConfig` and logs proxy
details after `configCache` is initialized (so `platform.json` values
are resolved alongside env vars)
- Logs `🌐 Proxy configuration active` with `http`, `https`, `noProxy`,
and `urlPatterns` when a proxy URL is present
  - Logs `Proxy is explicitly disabled` when `enabled: false` is set
  - Silent when no proxy is configured at all

## Example output

```json
{"component":"Server","level":"info","message":"🌐 Proxy configuration active","http":"(not set)","https":"http://proxy.example.com:8080","noProxy":"localhost,127.0.0.1","urlPatterns":"(all URLs proxied)"}
```

Fix solver bug: active count was halved after each append

After appending, the board gains live.length NEW cells on top of
the existing live.length active cells = live.length * 2 total.
Setting active = live.length (not *2) caused the solver to
declare victory when only half the cells were cleared, accepting
boards that are genuinely unsolvable.

commit.message:
fix: prevent stack overflow in Test262 CI with deep recursion

- Reduce MAX_CALL_STACK_DEPTH from 256 to 64 so the interpreter raises
  a JavaScript RangeError well before the native stack is exhausted.
  Each interpreter frame can consume several KiB of native stack space,
  and 64 levels is sufficient for any realistic JavaScript program.

- Add the stacker crate and wrap Interpreter::run() with
  stacker::maybe_grow(128KiB, 2MiB) so the native stack is dynamically
  extended via mmap/VirtualAlloc when headroom drops below 128 KiB.
  This is the same technique used by rustc itself and prevents
  SIGSEGV/STATUS_STACK_OVERFLOW on pathological inputs (e.g. Test262
  JSON.parse nesting tests, Proxy trap recursion).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore AI chat responses by fixing API key resolution

- Add EXPO_PUBLIC_OPENAI_API_KEY support so key is available in
  production builds on Vercel (where .env.local doesn't exist)
- Update app.config.js to check EXPO_PUBLIC_ prefixed env var first
- Replace silent template fallback with thrown errors so the UI
  shows clear error messages instead of fake responses
- Add console.error logging when API key is missing
- Show descriptive error messages in chat UI when AI fails

Root cause: On Vercel's build servers, .env.local is absent so
process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY is undefined. app.config.js evaluated
it as undefined, making Constants.expoConfig.extra.openaiApiKey
also undefined. All AI responses silently fell back to hardcoded
template text with no error indication.
